content.text:
Magnets are objects that create an invisible force — called MAGNETIC FORCE — that can attract or repel other objects without even touching them!

🧲 What magnets attract:
Magnets attract objects made of iron, steel, nickel, and cobalt. They do NOT attract plastic, wood, glass, rubber, or copper.

🔴🔵 Poles — Every magnet has two ends:
• North Pole (N)
• South Pole (S)

The law of magnetic poles:
✅ OPPOSITE poles attract — North attracts South
❌ SAME poles repel — North pushes away North

🌍 Earth is a Giant Magnet!
Our planet has a magnetic field. That's why a compass works — the compass needle is a tiny magnet that always points toward Earth's magnetic North Pole. Sailors and explorers have used compasses for over 1,000 years!

⚡ Electromagnets:
When electricity flows through a wire wrapped around iron, it creates a temporary magnet called an ELECTROMAGNET. You can turn it on and off! Electromagnets are used in cranes to lift cars, in electric motors, and in doorbells.

🔬 Magnetic force can work through materials:
A magnet can attract a paper clip through a piece of paper or even a thin piece of plastic — the force passes right through!

Magnetism is a fundamental force of nature, and it's used in everything from refrigerator magnets to MRI machines in hospitals.

diagrams.alt: Diagram showing magnetic poles, field lines, and examples of magnets attracting and repelling

experiment.instructions: Drag the magnet near different objects to see which ones it attracts!

combinations.resultLabel: The magnet attracts the iron nail! Iron contains iron atoms that line up with the magnetic field. The magnetic force pulls them together — without them even touching at first!

practiceQuestions.prompt: Which material will a magnet NOT attract?
practiceQuestions.hint: Magnets attract iron, steel, nickel, and cobalt — is wood on that list?

practiceQuestions.prompt: What happens when you bring two North poles of magnets together?

options.text: Earth's magnetic field pulling the compass needle

practiceQuestions.hint: A compass needle is a tiny magnet — what large magnet does it respond to?

options.text: Two poles — a North and a South

questions.prompt: An electromagnet is different from a regular magnet because:

options.text: It can be turned on and off using electricity

questions.prompt: Which of these is attracted to a magnet?
If a North pole and a South pole are brought close together, they will:
Why do sailors use a compass?

options.text: To measure how deep the ocean is
To find direction using Earth's magnetic field
